items.title: Malaysia to send diplomatic note to Philippines over Sabah claim
items.summary: REMBAU: Malaysia will send a diplomatic note to the Philippines to reject and protest any attempts to revive claims over Sabah, following remarks by Philippine Senator Robin Padilla. Foreign Minister Datuk Seri Mohamad Hasan said Wisma Putra would dispatch the note soon to reiterate Malaysia's firm stance. "We will send a diplomatic note to state our protest and rejection of those statements.

items.title: Riding out negative impact of Mideast conflict
items.summary: The impact is transmitted through higher oil prices, trade disruptions, financial market volatility, and rising living costs. However, rising costs and uncertainty pose a more pressing challenge. Higher oil prices are driving up transportation, production and import costs, particularly for food and industrial inputs.

items.title: SWCorp Pahang issues 238 notices for littering, 19 face court action
items.summary: SWCorp Pahang director Cairul Hisham Jalaluddin said 19 individuals have faced court action in Raub, Temerloh, and Kuantan. Today, 12 offenders underwent community service orders for periods ranging from one to four hours, starting at 9am. Cairul Hisham said SWCorp allows offenders to apply to serve their community service at locations near their homes to ease logistics and reduce travel costs.

items.title: Twice in a month: Lawyer caught driving Ford Mustang without road tax
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: A 27-year-old lawyer was caught driving a Ford Mustang without road tax or insurance coverage for the second time in a month yesterday. Kuala Lumpur Road Transport Department director Hamidi Adam said the man was stopped in the city centre while on his way home. Last year, Transport Minister Anthony Loke revealed that several prominent figures , including politicians, businessmen and even recipients of the "Tan Sri" title, were among vehicle owners who had failed to pay their road tax, with arrears amounting to RM35.7 million.

items.title: Road closure in Maran for Panguni Uthiram festival
items.summary: KUANTAN: The main road in front of the Sri Maranthandavar Temple in Sungai Jerik, Maran, and its surrounding areas will be closed from March 30 to April 1 for the Panguni Uthiram festival. The Maran district police, in a Facebook post, said the closure will take effect from 4pm on March 30 until 2pm on April 1. Access to the main road remains available via Jalan Simpang Bangas, continuing to Jalan Utama and Bandar Jengka.

items.title: Heatwave: Ministry rolls out four key measures to protect rice supply
items.summary: The measures include the implementation of the dry direct seeding method for padi, increasing the national rice buffer stock, conducting cloud seeding operations, and providing financial aid to affected farmers. Minister Datuk Seri Mohamad Sabu said that to mitigate the effects of the hot weather and boost dam levels, the ministry, through the Muda Agricultural Development Authority (Mada), will roll out the dry direct seeding technique. To ensure proactive future planning, Mohamad said the ministry's post-cabinet meeting last week approved the establishment of the National Padi and Rice Industry Consultative Committee (JKPIPB).

items.title: Housing Ministry eyes University of Cambridge tie-up on sustainable urban planning
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 28 — The Ministry of Housing and Local Government (KPKT) is seeking to establish a Malaysia-Cambridge urban platform to pursue Sustainable Development Goals (SDG) 2030 under the Madani economy framework. Its Minister, Nga Kor Ming, said the proposed collaboration with the University of Cambridge would strengthen strategic partnerships with leading global institutions in pursuit of sustainable urban development. Other priorities include advancing climate-resilient housing through transit-oriented development and nature-based solutions, enhancing land and planning coordination via a national urban observatory, and reviving abandoned housing projects.

items.title: Nga proposes Malaysia-Cambridge urban platform to drive SDG 2030 goals
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The Housing and Local Government Ministry is moving to establish a Malaysia-Cambridge urban platform to accelerate the nation's Sustainable Development Goals (SDG) 2030 under the Madani economy framework. Minister Nga Kor Ming said the proposed collaboration with the University of Cambridge aims to strengthen strategic partnerships with global institutions to drive sustainable urban development. Other priorities include advancing climate-resilient housing through transit-oriented development, enhancing planning via a national urban observatory, and reviving abandoned housing projects.

items.title: Indonesia enforces social media ban for under-16s
items.summary: JAKARTA: Indonesia has officially banned social media use for children under the age of 16, marking the start of its landmark digital safety regulation, PP Tunas. The Tata Kelola untuk Anak Aman dan Sehat Digital (Tunas) policy, which mandates the phased deactivation of underage accounts on high-risk platforms, makes Indonesia the first non-Western country to enforce such a nationwide restriction. The country's Communications and Digital Affairs Minister Meutya Hafid said the move reflects Indonesia's commitment to digital sovereignty and child protection, following a one-year transition period since the regulation was signed by President Prabowo Subianto.

items.title: Man arrested for selling fireworks at Terengganu Drawbridge with expired permit
items.summary: KUALA TERENGGANU: A 36-year-old man was arrested for selling firecrackers and fireworks without a valid permit at Dataran Drawbridge early this morning. Kuala Terengganu police chief Assistant Commissioner Azli Mohd Noor said the man was found openly selling the items to the public. Police seized various types of fireworks and firecrackers valued at RM4,000.

items.title: Traffic builds on major highways as Hari Raya travellers head home
items.summary: A spokesperson for the Malaysian Highway Authority, when contacted by Bernama, said congestion is currently concentrated along key stretches of the North-South Expressway and within the Klang Valley, while traffic on the East Coast Expressway remains manageable. “Meanwhile, on the North-South Expressway (E2), traffic heading north is slow from Senai Utara to Kulai, while southbound lanes are congested from Putra Mahkota to Nilai Utara,” he said. On the North-South Expressway Central Link (ELITE), southbound traffic is moving slowly from Bandar Serenia to Nilai Utara.

items.title: Melaka dam levels dip up to 30pct, but supply remains stable until April
items.summary: ALOR GAJAH: Water levels at Melaka's three main dams have dropped by between 10 and 30 per cent due to the ongoing dry spell and lack of rainfall. "Based on current reports, the Durian Tunggal Dam is at approximately 70 per cent, Jus Dam at 60 per cent, and Asahan Dam at 90 per cent. "The Malaysian Meteorological Department (MetMalaysia) has forecast high rainfall in the interior of the west coast, which covers the state's water catchment areas," he said at the Ayer Limau state constituency 'Aidilfitri Melaka Sayang Rakyat' event last night.

items.title: Concessionaire clarifies that fatal rider accident occurred on NPE, not LDP
items.summary: PETALING JAYA, March 28 — Amanat Lebuhraya Rakyat Berhad (ALR), the concessionaire of the Damansara-Puchong Expresswa (LDP), has issued a clarification regarding a fatal accident involving a food delivery rider on Thursday, saying this did not occur on its expressway as previously reported. In a statement, ALR confirmed that the accident took place at KM 3.0 of the New Pantai Expressway (NPE), on the stretch heading from Bandar Sunway towards Bangsar. The clarification addresses earlier reports that cited Petaling Jaya deputy police chief Supt M. Hussin Sollehuddin Zolkifly as saying the LDP was the location of the incident.

items.title: Misusing subsidies a betrayal of the country, says KPDN as enforcement tightens
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, March 28 — The Ministry of Domestic Trade and Cost of Living (KPDN) has issued a stern warning that any attempt by individuals or syndicates to take advantage of the current global conflict will not be tolerated. Its Enforcement director-general Datuk Azman Adam said misappropriating national subsidies is a betrayal of the people and the country. At the same time, 2,400 KPDN enforcement officers nationwide are on high alert to carry out inspections and monitoring activities on the ground.

items.title: Kelantan rice yields to plunge 40pct due to prolonged drought
items.summary: Kelantan Farmers Association (Pesak) chairman Zuha Ismail said existing water supplies are insufficient to irrigate the affected padi fields. He said the drought impacts 26,000 hectares of land worked by over 5,000 farmers. The current crop is aged between 85 and 120 days, with some farmers already beginning the harvesting process.

items.title: Silent killer: Miros warns against sleeping in cars with engine running
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Sleeping in a car with the engine and air conditioning running, even for a short nap, can be fatal due to the silent threat of carbon monoxide (CO) poisoning. Malaysian Institute of Road Safety Research (Miros) director-general Associate Professor Dr Siti Zaharah Ishak warned that CO gas can seep into the cabin through leaks in the engine bay or exhaust system. The risk increases significantly in enclosed or poorly ventilated areas, such as multi-storey or underground car parks, where exhaust fumes accumulate and raise CO concentrations around the vehicle.

items.title: KPDN steps up enforcement to curb smuggling, safeguard fuel supply
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The Domestic Trade and Cost of Living Ministry (KPDN) will continue to tighten enforcement to curb leakages and smuggling, while ensuring the smooth supply of controlled goods amid geopolitical uncertainties arising from the conflict in the Middle East. KPDN Enforcement director-general Datuk Azman Adam said the ministry's commitment is in line with the prime minister's call to ensure the sustainability of the nation's energy supply to meet the needs of the people. As the ministry responsible for the supply chain of controlled goods, he said KPDN is formulating more aggressive strategies to address potential disruptions in the global fuel supply that could directly impact the economy.

items.title: Three Melaka dams see levels drop due to heat, state assures supply until April
items.summary: ALOR GAJAH, March 28 — Water levels at three dams in Melaka have dropped between 10 and 30 per cent due to hot weather and lack of rain recently. State Works, Infrastructure, Public Facilities and Transport Committee chairman Datuk Hameed Mytheen Kunju Basheer, however, said that the water level remains at a normal reading and, so far, the water supply is expected to be sufficient for the state until April. “Based on the Malaysian Meteorological Department (MetMalaysia) report, high rainfall is expected in the interior of the west coast, covering water catchment areas in this state,” he told reporters after the Ayer Limau state assembly ‘Aidilfitri Melaka Sayang Rakyat’ event here last night.

items.title: Traffic builds up on major highways as holidaymakers return
items.summary: A Malaysian Highway Authority (LLM) spokesperson said congestion is currently concentrated along key stretches of the North-South Expressway (Plus) and within the Klang Valley, while traffic on the East Coast Expressway (LPT) remains manageable. "On the North-South Expressway (E1), northbound traffic is slow from Permatang Pauh to Sungai Dua, while southbound congestion is reported from Juru Auto City to the Juru Toll Plaza and from Changkat Jering to Kuala Kangsar. On the North-South Expressway Central Link (Elite), southbound traffic is crawling from Bandar Serenia to Nilai Utara.

items.title: Man arrested over cooking gas cylinder theft
items.summary: MIRI: Police have arrested a man over the theft of cooking gas cylinders following a raid at Jalan Padang Kerbau yesterday evening. Miri police chief Assistant Commissioner Mohd Farhan Lee said the case is being probed under Section 379 of the Penal Code for theft, an offence punishable by imprisonment for up to seven years, a fine, or both. Police have also advised the public to remain vigilant and report any criminal activities.

items.title: Socso plan to cover cross-border workers commuting to Singapore in final stage, says HR minister
items.summary: Minister R. Ramanan said the initiative is part of broader efforts to strengthen the country’s social safety net. “In addition, we are also finalising a study to extend social protection to mothers during childbirth,” he said in a statement yesterday. Reflecting on his first 100 days in office, Ramanan said Kesuma has prioritised policies that deliver direct and long-term benefits to the people, particularly in social protection, skills development and worker welfare.

items.title: Level 2 heatwave hits Kedah, Perlis and Hulu Perak, says MetMalaysia
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 28 — Five districts in Kedah, Hulu Perak in Perak and the whole of Perlis recorded a Level 2 heatwave, according to the Malaysian Meteorological Department (MetMalaysia). Meanwhile, several other areas in Kedah — Bandar Baharu, Kulim, Kuala Muda, Padang Terap, Kubang Pasu and Langkawi — are under Level 1 (alert) status. A Level 2 heatwave is declared when the daily maximum temperatures range from 37 to 40 degrees Celsius for at least three consecutive days, while Level 1 refers to daily temperatures between 35 and 37 degrees Celsius over the same period.

items.title: The truth is out there: US vice-president 'obsessed' with UFOs
items.summary: WASHINGTON: US Vice President JD Vance vowed Friday to plumb US government information about unidentified flying objects, adding that in his view, aliens are in fact demons. "I've still got three more years as vice president. Johnson had asked about President Donald Trump's order to federal agencies in February to begin "identifying and releasing" government files related to UFOs and aliens.

items.title: Call for special BNPL act to shield youths from spiralling debt
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The enactment of a special act governing "Buy Now Pay Later" (BNPL) schemes is critical to protecting consumers, particularly the younger generation, from spiralling debt. Global Law Thinker Society (GLTS) Malaysia chairman Muhammad Nazrin Faiz said the move is essential to balance consumer needs with the interests of traders. He said the enactment could also benefit both parties where traders can continue to generate profits, while consumers can spend without facing financial risks.

items.title: No injuries in rooftop explosion, fire in downtown Toronto
items.summary: TORONTO: A pair of explosions on the roof of a downtown Toronto building prompted an emergency response on Friday but no one was injured, officials said. Deepak Chagger, a spokesperson with Toronto Fire Services, said the fire broke out in an area of the roof where construction work was taking place. Fire crews extinguished the fire and there were no injuries reported, Chagger said.

items.title: HR Ministry plans protection for over 400k Malaysians working in Singapore
items.summary: Minister Datuk Seri R. Ramanan said the "Skim Kembara" initiative is part of a broader effort to strengthen social protection, which includes a study in its final stages to extend coverage to women in confinement. Marking his 100th day in office, Ramanan said he is focused on ensuring policies improve lives through social protection, skills development, and worker welfare. Ramanan said the collaboration aims to secure job placements for 5,000 veterans through the MyFutureJobs Wira Career Protection Programme by 2030.

items.title: #NSTviral: One parked car brings traffic to a halt as trailer gets stuck
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: A single illegally parked car was all it took to bring an entire stretch of road to a standstill, sparking outrage online. The obstruction was caused by a black car abandoned on the road shoulder, right at the curve, effectively choking the passage for the heavy vehicle. Within minutes, traffic began piling up in both directions, with frustrated motorists left stranded in a standstill jam.

items.title: Social media age limit a 'digital seatbelt' for Malaysian youth
items.summary: He noted that the measure complements the government's earlier efforts requiring social media platforms to obtain operating licenses starting January 2025, aimed at enhancing service providers' accountability for user safety. "This move ensures that social media providers operate more responsibly, prioritising the safety and well-being of their users. Restricting those under 16 from registering or maintaining social media accounts is like a 'digital seatbelt,' offering an added layer of protection for young users," he said.

items.title: #BellaSyedYes: Syed Saddiq and Bella Astillah confirm engagement, after proposal on Mount Kinabalu (VIDEO)
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 28 — After months of playful denials, coy social media posts, and a trail of clues that kept fans on the edge of their seats, it appears that Muar MP Syed Saddiq Syed Abdul Rahman and actress-singer Bella Astillah have finally taken the next big step in their relationship. The speculation had reached its peak on March 27 when Syed Saddiq's brother, influencer Space Dollah (Syed Abdullah Syed Abdul Rahman), seemingly confirmed the engagement during a live-streaming session. Bella's response?

items.title: US media: FBI director personal email hacked by Iran-linked group
items.summary: WASHINGTON: An Iran-linked hacker group has accessed an email account of FBI Director Kash Patel and published personal photos and other material online, US media reported Friday. CNN and other US media outlets, citing sources familiar with the breach, said the hack of Patel's email was claimed by the Handala Hack Team, a pro-Iranian hacktivist group. The FBI and Justice Department did not immediately respond to a request for comment.

items.title: Apple hires ex-Google executive to head AI marketing amid push to improve Siri
items.summary: SAN FRANCISCO: Apple on Friday said it has hired Lilian Rincon, who previously spent nearly a decade at Google overseeing its shopping and assistant products, as the vice president of product marketing for artificial intelligence, reporting to its marketing chief Greg "Joz" Joswiak. The hire comes as Apple is readying an improved version of Siri, its virtual assistant, for release this year, rebuilt with technology from Alphabet's Gemini AI model. © New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

items.title: More than 300 US troops injured since start of Iran war
items.summary: WASHINGTON: More than 300 US troops have been wounded since the start of the Iran war on Feb 28, US Central Command said on Friday. "Since the start of Operation Epic Fury, approximately 303 US service members have been wounded. A US official who asked not to be identified told AFP that 10 troops remain seriously wounded.

items.title: Retired navy man nets RM6,000 monthly from backyard poultry farm
items.summary: Hamdan Ahmad Sapli, 58, is earning up to RM6,000 a month by rearing laying hens and cultivating various crops. [[nid:1405123]] By August 2022, his hens began producing eggs. Hamdan chose laying hens for their low maintenance and minimal space requirements.

items.title: Police rescue 14, arrest seven in Melaka human trafficking raids
items.summary: MELAKA, March 28 — Police detained seven individuals for alleged involvement in human trafficking and rescued 14 others believed to be victims of forced labour here today. Dzulkhairi said that in the same operation, police also rescued 14 victims aged between 25 and 36, comprising seven men and six women from the Philippines, as well as one woman from Myanmar. He said initial investigations found that all the victims were believed to have been trafficked for forced labour exploitation in online scam activities.

items.title: Body of teenage boy found after suspected crocodile attack
items.summary: KUCHING, March 28 — The body of a teenage boy who was suspected to have been a victim of a crocodile attack while bathing with his family along Sungai Sarawak in Kampung Segubang, Batu Kawa, was found this afternoon. The Sarawak Fire and Rescue Operations Centre said that the body of the boy, 15, was found by family members during a search and rescue operation at about 5.30 pm not far from where he went missing. “The body of the victim was found without his left and right arms,” the centre said, adding that the body has been handed over to the police for further action.

items.title: Colgate must face lawsuits over safety of mouth rinse for young children
items.summary: The judge dismissed a similar lawsuit concerning Colgate toothpaste. Consumers in the proposed class actions said US health authorities warned that children under 6 should not use fluoride rinses, and recommended that children between 2 and 6 use only "pea-sized" amounts of fluoride toothpaste. They said Colgate's packaging, featuring bright colors and flavors such as Bubble Fruit and Silly Strawberry, made it seem safe for young children to use rinses, and to use as much toothpaste as older children and adults.

items.title: Perak Mufti Department: 'Tidur Lambak' event may breach Islamic law
items.summary: IPOH: The Perak Mufti Department has expressed concern over the "Tidur Lambak" event, citing risks of "ikhtilat" or the mingling of unrelated men and women, which could contravene Islamic law. The two-day event, organised by Tapir Studio, is described as an immersive experience reimagining the communal sleeping arrangements typically found during "balik kampung" trips. In a statement, the department said while it did not reject the event entirely, the programme must be reviewed to ensure compliance with Islamic principles and Malaysian cultural values.

items.title: Night Zoo Johor draws record 7,330 visitors on opening night
items.summary: JOHOR BARU: Night Zoo Johor saw a massive turnout on its opening night yesterday, with 7,330 visitors experiencing the state's newest attraction. The night zoo features 35 additional species comprising 100 animals. Night Zoo Johor operates from Friday to Sunday, 8pm to 11pm, with the last entry at 10pm.

items.title: Health Ministry aims to ease HKL MRI congestion with increased outsourcing
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 28 — The Ministry of Health (MOH) has reintroduced the Hospital Services Outsourcing Programme (HSOP) at Kuala Lumpur Hospital (HKL) to ease congestion and reduce waiting times for patients requiring magnetic resonance imaging (MRI) services. In a statement yesterday, HKL said the HSOP reduced patient waiting times by 33 per cent in 2025, from 24 weeks to 16 weeks. “In 2025, HKL optimised 96.8 per cent of its allocated slots, using 365 out of 377.

items.title: Heatwave sparks surge in air conditioner sales as consumers seek relief
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The extreme heatwave currently sweeping the country has triggered a surge in air conditioner sales, with some units reportedly sold out as consumers scramble for relief. A check by Harian Metro found that some desperate homeowners are purchasing up to five units at a time to cool their residences. As a result, I had to buy an air conditioner," he said.

items.title: Speeding trailer in Port Klang triggers viral chaos as container falls, creating traffic snarl
items.summary: SHAH ALAM, March 28 — Traffic was congested along Jalan Kem in Port Klang, near here, yesterday after a container fell off a trailer believed to have been speeding. South Klang district police chief ACP Lim Jit Huey said the incident, which occurred at about noon, did not result in any casualties or damage to other vehicles. “Initial investigations found that the trailer was believed to be speeding when passing through the location, causing the container to become detached from the trailer before falling onto the road.

items.title: Merbok murder victims laid to rest
items.summary: SUNGAI PETANI: The couple who were found murdered and dismembered in their home in Kampung Belau, Merbok, were laid to rest last night. The remains of Zikri Abdul Rahman, 65, and Suraya Man, 53, were buried at the Masjid Al Atiq Islamic Cemetery in Merbok at 9pm. It was previously reported that three family members were found dead in the house two days ago.

items.title: Merbok murder victim couple laid to rest
items.summary: SUNGAI PETANI, March 28 — The remains of a married couple believed murdered in Kampung Belau, Merbok, were laid to rest at 9pm last night at the Masjid Al-Atiq Muslim cemetery here. Two Malaysian Armed Forces (ATM) vehicles carrying the remains of Zikri Abdul Rahman, 65, and his wife Suraya Man, 53, arrived at the mosque at 7 pm, after being claimed by their family from Hospital Sultanah Bahiyah in Alor Setar. About 300 people, comprising relatives and villagers, attended the burial ceremony, which began after Maghrib prayers.

items.title: Three Thai nationals arrested for attempting to smuggle petrol, diesel into Thailand
items.summary: KANGAR, March 28 — The General Operations Force (GOF) detained three Thai men who were attempting to smuggle petrol and diesel into Thailand using modified vehicle tanks on Jalan Kangar-Padang Besar, near the Padang Besar Immigration, Customs, Quarantine and Security Complex (ICQS) yesterday. Its Northern Brigade Commander, SAC Balveer Singh a/l Mahindar Singh said the police arrested the men, aged between 22 and 45, after inspecting three pickup trucks during ‘Op Taring Bravo 1’ at 9am. "As a result of the inspection, it was found that there was a quantity of liquid, suspected to be petrol and diesel, in the modified tanks of each vehicle, believed to be for smuggling petrol and diesel into Thailand," he said in a statement last night.

items.title: Wangsa Maju scandal: As video of alleged extortion goes viral PKR tells two implicated members to step down from posts
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 28 — Two individuals from the Wangsa Maju Federal Territory Residents’ Representative Council (MPPWP) have been ordered to immediately vacate their positions following a viral video alleging they solicited protection money from a business premises. Federal Territories PKR Leadership Council (MPN) chairman Datuk Azman Abidin said the decision against R. Vijeyndran and G. Paarthiydasan was reached during a Federal Territories PKR Political Bureau meeting yesterday. “The Political Bureau has also directed the Federal Territories PKR Disciplinary Committee to conduct a thorough internal investigation and report its findings to the bureau in the near future,” he said in a statement yesterday.

items.title: Jakarta visit: PM Anwar and Prabowo address West Asia tensions
items.summary: JAKARTA, March 28 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim concluded his special visit to Jakarta yesterday after meeting Indonesian President Prabowo Subianto at the Istana Merdeka here. Following the discussions, Prabowo accompanied Anwar to Halim Perdanakusuma Airport as a gesture of goodwill and cordial ties between the two leaders. Anwar departed for home at about 7.15pm local time, concluding his one-day working visit.

items.title: Perhilitan clarifies 19 elephants on loan for conservation, not sale
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, March 27 — The allegation that the Department of Wildlife and National Parks (Perhilitan) sold 19 elephants to certain parties, as circulated on social media, is false and misleading. Perhilitan director-general (DG) Datuk Abdul Kadir Abu Hashim said the elephants in question were not sold but instead are being transferred on loan for ongoing wildlife conservation and management. He said this collaboration also opens up opportunities for technology and expertise transfer, particularly in the fields of controlled breeding, health monitoring, nutrition and modern habitat management.

items.title: Penang considers WFH for civil servants, waits for word from Putrajaya
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN, March 27 — The Penang state government is studying the implementation of working from home (WFH) for civil servants following challenges arising from rising fuel prices, particularly diesel, amid the West Asia crisis. He said the implementation of WFH is a measure to reduce the burden of operating costs and daily travel following the increase in petrol and diesel prices. Chow said the rise in diesel prices also has an indirect impact on the government sector, including local authorities in Penang, such as solid waste management services that depend entirely on diesel.

items.title: Father escapes, four-year-old burnt to death in Volvo lorry fire in Pasir Gudang
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, March 27 — A four-year-old boy was burnt to death after the lorry he was travelling in with an adult man, believed to be his father, caught fire at Iskandar Halal Park, Cahaya Baru, Pasir Gudang, here this morning. Pasir Gudang Fire and Rescue Station operations commander Iszahwati Supardi said they received an emergency call regarding the incident at 10.46 am, and six personnel with a Fire Rescue Tender were dispatched to the scene. Firefighters subsequently extinguished the fire.

items.title: Viral video sparks KL police probe into alleged extortion by FT reps in Wangsa Maju
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 27 — Police have received three reports over a viral video showing two individuals, suspected to be members of the Federal Territories Residents’ Representative Council (MPP), allegedly soliciting protection money from a massage centre in Wangsa Maju. Kuala Lumpur police chief Datuk Fadil Marsus said the case is being investigated under Section 385 of the Penal Code for extortion, as well as Section 233 of the Communications and Multimedia Act 1998 related to the dissemination of content. Recently, closed-circuit television footage went viral showing two men introducing themselves as MPP members while asking for money from a massage centre.

items.title: Perlis, parts of Kedah and Perak hit heatwave Level 2, 13 other areas in peninsula at Level 1
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 27 — The whole of Perlis, along with four districts in Kedah and one in Perak, are at heatwave level 2, while 13 other areas in peninsular Malaysia are at level 1. The 13 areas at heatwave level 1 are Padang Terap, Kubang Pasu, Pulau Langkawi, Kuala Muda, Sik, Kulim and Bandar Baharu in Kedah, Timur Laut and Utara (Penang), as well as Selama, Kinta, Kuala Kangsar and Perak Tengah (Perak). A heatwave level 2 is declared when the maximum daily temperature is between 37° to 40° Celsius for a period of at least three consecutive days, while heatwave level 1 is a maximum daily temperature of 35° to 37°Celsius Celsius for at least three consecutive days.

items.title: Malaysia’s medicine stock stable for now, but govt’s main supplier sets up task force over disruption risk
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 27 — Malaysia’s medicine supply remains stable for the next few months, even as global trade disruptions caused by the conflict in West Asia threaten pharmaceutical supply chains worldwide. He added that the situation underscores the need to strengthen Malaysia’s domestic pharmaceutical manufacturing capabilities to ensure long-term medicine security. Zulkifli also emphasised the importance of enhancing Malaysia’s domestic pharmaceutical manufacturing capabilities.

items.title: MACC seeks whistleblowers as RM10m bribery claims involving politician swirl
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 27 — The Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) has urged any party with credible information regarding the alleged soliciting and paying of money to a politician to come forward and lodge an official report. In a statement today, the MACC said an official report must be made to enable a thorough, transparent and fact-based investigation to be carried out. “The MACC also takes note of various allegations circulating in the media (regarding this case) and to date, no official report has been received by the MACC in relation to these claims, and the information that has gone viral has yet to be verified,” it said.

items.title: Malaysian Armed Forces expands use of drones in fight against fuel smuggling at borders
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 27 — The Malaysian Armed Forces (MAF) is strengthening operational readiness by deploying assets and resources comprehensively in all border areas of the country to combat the diversion and leakage of fuel subsidies. The MAF also strengthens strategic cooperation with various enforcement agencies through real-time information sharing and the implementation of integrated operations, including intensified dynamic patrols in border areas and fuel distribution points. The cooperation involves the Royal Malaysia Police, the Malaysian Border Control and Protection Agency, the Royal Malaysian Customs Department, the Malaysian Maritime Enforcement Agency and other security agencies.

items.title: Anwar arrives in Indonesia for talks with Prabowo on Middle East crisis and bilateral ties
items.summary: JAKARTA, March 27 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim arrived in Jakarta on Friday afternoon for a special visit to discuss escalating geopolitical developments in West Asia and strengthen Malaysia-Indonesia bilateral cooperation. The aircraft carrying Anwar landed at Halim Perdanakusuma Airport in East Jakarta at 3.45pm local time. The Prime Minister was received by Indonesian Foreign Minister Sugiono, Indonesia’s Ambassador to Malaysia Raden Datuk Mohammad Iman Hascarya and Malaysia’s Ambassador to Indonesia Datuk Syed Mohamad Hasrin Tengku Hussin.

items.title: English text of Federal Constitution prevails until Agong says otherwise, Putrajaya tells courts
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 27 — The English version of the Federal Constitution is the nation’s authoritative legal document until the Yang di-Pertuan Agong declares otherwise, Putrajaya has asserted in its official defence against a lawsuit seeking to elevate the Bahasa Melayu version. It cited two landmark Federal Court rulings in the cases of Indira Gandhi and Loh Siew Hong as binding precedents that have already settled the matter. The lawsuit was filed last year by lawyer Haniff Khatri Abdulla, who is seeking a court declaration that the Bahasa Melayu version of the constitution should take precedence.

items.title: Karambunai state polls result stands as Election Court throws out Warisan challenge
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU, March 27 — The Election Court on Friday struck out a Warisan candidate’s election petition seeking to nullify the Karambunai constituency election result in the 17th Sabah state election. High Court judge Wong Siong Tung, via Zoom, allowed all preliminary objections raised by the respondents to strike out the election petition filed against them. The petitioner, Ahmad Jais Otong, named the returning officer (RO) for the Karambunai state constituency election, the Election Commission (EC) of Malaysia and Perikatan Nasional’s PAS candidate, Datuk Aliakbar Gulasan, as the first, second and third respondents, respectively.

items.title: Driver dies after Isuzu D-Max plunges into drain in Sandakan crash
items.summary: The victim, identified as Chock Vui Heong, 50, was pronounced dead at the scene by medical personnel. Sandakan Fire and Rescue Station chief Jimmy Lagung said the department received an emergency call at 12.56pm before a team was dispatched to the location about seven kilometres away. He said the victim was extricated using special equipment, including hydraulic rescue tools, but was confirmed dead at the scene before the body was sent to hospital, while operations ended at 1.58pm after ensuring the area was safe.

items.title: KK court fixes May 25 mention in Shafie Apdal contempt proceedings linked to Zara Qairina inquest
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU, March 27 — The Sessions Court has fixed May 25 for a mention in the committal proceedings involving former chief minister Datuk Seri Mohd Shafie Apdal to receive an update on a judicial review application seeking to quash the inquest into the death of Zara Qairina Mahathir. Shafie is alleged to have made remarks concerning the inquest that may amount to contempt of court, while the court had previously granted a stay of the committal proceedings pending updates on the judicial review. The court had earlier granted the Attorney General leave to commence contempt proceedings against Shafie and activist Che Muna Din, while a judicial review application filed by Pertubuhan Kebajikan Ekonomi Semangat Bersatu Malaysia challenging the inquest proceedings is opposed by the Attorney General’s Chambers, with respective counsel representing the parties.

items.title: MetMalaysia issues storm alert for parts of Sabah and Sarawak until 6pm
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 27 — The Malaysian Meteorological Department (MetMalaysia) has issued a warning of thunderstorms, heavy rain and strong winds in Sabah and Sarawak until 6pm today. MetMalaysia said in a statement that Kapit (Belaga) and Limbang in Sarawak are affected. Meanwhile, the Interior (Sipitang and Beaufort) and West Coast (Papar and Penampang) in Sabah are also affected.
